TextENGNew: ...Tomoe awoke, freezing cold.
The area around her was pitch black, so she couldn't see anything. ...How strange, she thought. It was sunny until a little while ago... Did she fall asleep?
...A fishy aroma began to uncomfortably tickle her nose.
She wondered how long she was asleep.
And more importantly... where was she?
When Tomoe got up to take a look around... she noticed her body felt as heavy as lead.
Then,
Lukewarm liquid was overflowing from the back of her throat... suffocating her.
Her right cheek felt sore in the place where it was resting on the ground.
It was only after feeling the surface of the asphalt... that Tomoe finally remembered.
(...Right... I was stabbed...)
...It happened so quickly, she didn't have an opportunity to defend herself.

Tomoe came to the service area to refuel her car, used their office to take care of some things, and was about to head for the exit when she noticed a woman crossing her arms calling for her to stop, so Tomoe slammed on the brakes.
The woman said that she was having car trouble, and that her battery had died.
Tomoe initially felt some desire to hurry out of there, but thinking it wouldn't be too much trouble, she parked her car in the adjacent parking space.
Then, as soon as she opened the hood of the car and connected the jumper cables,
The strength in Tomoe's legs suddenly gave out and she collapsed to the ground.
Before she could comprehend what happened... she felt a burning shock on her right side.
At the same time, she could hear the sound of a sticky liquid pouring out.
"Gggh... Aaah...!!"
Even as her face distorted in the intense pain... Tomoe lifted her head to see the face of the person who stabbed her.
The sun was behind her, so it was difficult to see her expression... but her mouth was stained bright red.
Smirking... with a devilish smile...
Cold.
Even though her whole body was hot enough to burn... it felt cold.
Her coughing grew louder and more intense... and she suffered pain like something was bludgeoning her lungs.
Her heart was beating terribly loud.
And the rhythm was... little by little, getting weaker.
She focused all her power into her eyes... blinking over and over to help her see into the darkness.
Then, in her blurry vision... Tomoe could make out the bright, scratched surface of her car... with a fuzzy outline in the driver's seat.
It looked like... the car had been robbed.
The glove compartment was left open, documents were scattered about, and the seat cushion was torn open.
They probably took the wallet and coins... but there was a good chance they took all the documents related to the case too.
Nevertheless.
Tomoe slowly pulled her right arm out from underneath her chest... looked at the thing still held in her bloody hand... and felt relieved.
Just this... Right, just keeping this from them was good enough...
(That's why... I got stabbed in the end... Ahaha, how careless of me...)
Moreover... she said that a detective's top priority was their duties... and yet this was what she adhered to at the end of things.
She laughed... while thinking it was absurd...
That girl   is probably going to cry.
No.   I'm sure she'll be angry.
At how in the end   her big sis protected something so foolish.
I'm　Sorry.
I couldn't be   a   good   big   sister.
Even so... she had to tell them.
She heard a little of what the culprit said... and had to tell them at least that...
Faintly.
From somewhere, she could hear the sound of a siren, and she could see a blinking red light.
As she listened to that, somewhere in her consciousness... Tomoe was working hard, refusing to rest. Even as her body grew heavier, she desperately struggled to remember.
...Repeating the words burned into her ears, panting as she tried to squeeze the words out of the back of her throat... repeating it many times as it suffocated her.
"...Shi...ro... cabinet... off...ice..."
And at last... she barely managed to spit the words out, mixed with bubbles of blood. But before she could check if her words had gotten through to the person who found her...
...Her eyelids grew heavy... and calmly closed...
...I came as soon as I heard the news, and I found Chief Yamaoki waiting in front of the operating room.
No, not just him. ...On the chair next to him, I could see Madoka-san with her head hanging down.
"Madoka-chan..."
Madoka-san squeezed both her hands together and tried to respond to Ooishi-san's call with a smile, as tears streamed down her face.
After gently rubbing her back, Chief Yamaoki stood up and turned to us.
"...What's the current situation?"

"...@k@vS08/30/321300038. She was bleeding heavily when they found her, and she was already unconscious when they brought her in.@k@vS08/30/321300039. Her liver was also damaged... so the situation is still unpredictable."
"N-no way...!"
"Does... does she need blood?@k@vS08/11/321100603. I have the same blood type. Please use as much as you need!!"
"There's no need for that...@k@vS08/30/321300041. Just the sentiment... is enough."
We didn't need to hear anything else... to understand what he meant by that.
"...I-it's all right... all right...!"
Madoka-san muttered that in a weak, trembling voice.

"Sis has always been tough enough to recover through serious injuries...@k@vS08/29/321200278. Pulled muscles, sprains... any serious injury healed quickly...!@k@vS08/29/321200279. So even now, she'll be all right... definitely...!"
"Yes, that's right.@k@vS08/11/321100606. ...Of course it is.@k@vS08/11/321100607. ...Definitely...!"
Ooishi-san's words of comfort... were probably also directed at himself.
Hearing him... saying those few words of comfort while desperately trying to suppress the intense emotions that were about to explode with a few words... only hurt more.
"...Chief Yamaoki.@k@vS08/13/321000711. Do we know anything about the culprit?"
"Since her wallet and cash were taken out of her car, we are mobilizing the First and Second Investigation Divisions under the assumption this was a car robbery."
"...At the time she was discovered, she was holding only this one thing and refused to let go."
Then Chief Yamaoki showed us... a bloody bankbook in a plastic bag.
"What is this...?"

"She'd likely been saving up money there for a while now.@k@vS08/30/321300045. According to Hayami-kun of the Mobile Investigation Team, the worst wound she suffered... likely occurred when the suspect stabbed her in an attempt to take it."
I took the book from Chief Yamaoki and looked at the front cover.
...The name "Madoka Minai" was written on the bankbook.
"Why does this have Madoka-san's name...?"
"It seems she kept it in her handbag and carried it around.@k@vS08/30/321300047. ...I suppose she also had a wallet and other things in there, but this was the only thing she tried to take back..."
"Ridiculous..."
A single word.
Unable to hold back any longer, Madoka-san began spilling tears as she muttered that one word.

"Seeing that, I can't help but laugh...@k@vS08/29/321200282. That account was opened 10 years ago... The first thing she put in it... was all the money a relative of ours gave Sis to help pay for her tuition..."
"To throw away her life... for that sort of pocket change... Ahaha, aahahaha...!"
"...That's not all.@k@vS08/29/321200285. On top of that, she deposited money in the account every month... like her scholarship money, and allowance from relatives..."
"Even after I ran away from home, and even after we reconciled... she never forgot...!!"
"Madoka-chan..."

"I don't need this... I don't need it!@k@vS08/29/321200288. I don't need this money... at all!@k@vS08/29/321200289. ...When she wakes up, I'm going to smack her and return it...!!@k@vS08/29/321200290. So... please... pleeease...!!"
She was holding her hands together in prayer, so I couldn't say anything to her... I looked up at the "surgery in progress" light above the operating room door and saw that it was lit in red.
And then,
The light suddenly turned off, and a female doctor dressed in surgical clothes quickly emerged from inside. Then,
"...Are any of you family members?"
"...Ah, yes."
"...Please come right away.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_docFa1003. It's over..."
After that cruel sentence, Madoka-san let out a small scream and rushed into the operating room.
Then...
"...Ah, aaaaaahh, Siiiiis!!!@k@vS08/29/321200296. Uwaaah... ah, aaaaaaahh...!!"
Madoka-san was... wailing.
I could hear it from the operating room... the lengthy electronic beep of the EKG.
Absolutely merciless... such a harsh way of telling the tale...
"...Damn it... Daaamn iiit...!!"
"Yes, I've never been here before.@k@vS08/28/320500966. Isn't that obvious?"
"...Is that a problem?!"
"The Chief is a bit of a nitwit but everyone else at my station is excellent.@k@vS08/28/320500969. Just last month, we received an award from the general manager."

"It may be improper of me to say this since I've never been a parent myself,@k@vS08/28/320500971. but now I can understand... that my memories of my father aren't limited to just the time we spent together.@k@vS08/28/320500972. Please don't let it get to you."
Always so direct... and with a sense of responsibility and pride in her heart.
Never letting her past wounds hold her back... and always showing compassion for others.
It's too soon... A person like that shouldn't die so young!
It can't be true...! There's no way it can end like this...!!
I suddenly looked back after hearing a boisterous noise.
...And I saw Ooishi-san banging his fist against the wall... groaning as he clenched his teeth in rage.
"O-Ooishi-san..."
"Sorry, Akasaka-san...!@k@vS08/11/321100610. Could you be quiet for a moment...?!"
"But Ooishi-san, your hand is bleedi..."
"I told you to be quieeet!!!"
Ooishi-san let out an angry roar...the likes of which I hadn't heard even back in the kidnapping case.
But that anger wasn't directed at me... it was for the culprit. And then,
"Haha, hahahahah...!@k@vS08/11/321100613. Akasaka-san... Do you know what the villagers of Hinamizawa used to call me...?"
"They called me "the messenger of Oyashiro-sama"... Haha, hahahah...@k@vS08/11/321100615. ...They said that any person I contacted would die a horrible death or go missing..."
"But as far as I know, that only applied to one person... So I laughed it off as unwarranted slander..."

"But... even so!@k@vS08/11/321100618. Ever since I got here, everyone I've investigated is dying!@k@vS08/11/321100619. Everyone!@k@vS08/11/321100620. As if to mock me!!@k@vS08/11/321100621. ...And this time, it's Chief Inspector Minai!!"
"She shouldn't have died. She had nothing to do with Hinamizawa!!@k@vS08/11/321100623. It's all because I asked her to cooperate!!@k@vS08/11/321100624. ...God damn it!!"
"Why?! Why is it?!@k@vS08/11/321100626. If they had to kill someone, it should have been me!!@k@vS08/11/321100627. Why are they killing everyone except meee?!@k@vS08/11/321100628. Uuuooooohh!!!"
"St-stop it! Ooishi-san!@k@vS08/13/321000726. Do you really think she would want you hurting yourself?!"
His fist was covered in blood after repeatedly beating the wall in anger... and he had started banging his head against the wall until I shouted at him to stop.
In response, he stared back at me with all his rage abundantly displayed across his face... but then he took a deep breath and slowly returned to himself.
...Ooishi-san continued trembling for a while, unable to completely suppress his emotions... but he eventually noticed the blood on the wall and his shoulders slumped down.
"Ooishi-san..."
"...I'm sorry, Akasaka-san.@k@vS08/11/321100632. I should really act my age... No, I'm ashamed of myself..."
"No... I understand how you feel, Ooishi-san.@k@vS08/13/321000729. I'd like to think I do."
Ooishi-san had doted and placed his hopes on Chief Inspector Minai as if she were his own daughter.
So it was only natural he'd be so shocked that she died before him.
On top of that... being caught up in an unexpected incident, and suffering such a violent death...!
The frustration and the hatred... the passion boiling inside me started to flare up.
...If God really exists.
Why was she given such a terrible ending?
She worked harder than anyone, she suffered so much... She and her sister were finally about to find happiness together, so why?!
"...Chief Yamaoki."
"What is it?"

"I realize that I'm an outsider, but would you please allow me to assist in finding the killer?@k@vS08/13/321000733. I don't know if I'll be much help, but I think I can balance out the number of investigators..."
I was completely aware that this was outside my jurisdiction. ...But I just couldn't overlook this incident.
If I could, I'd like to catch the killer with my own hands and tear them apart!!
Even if that was impossible, I could show them a Hell fitting for the crime they committed!!
The vengeance in my heart was burning up... I couldn't hold it back as it enveloped my entire body.
...However.

"By all means,@k@vS08/30/321300050. ...is what I'd like to say, Inspector Akasaka.@k@vS08/30/321300051. But I believe you have other responsibilities.@k@vS08/30/321300052. Didn't you come to this town to uncover the secrets of the Great Hinamizawa Disaster?"
"B-but... this is different from that!@k@vS08/13/321000735. Plus, she did a lot to support us..."

"They're actually one and the same.@k@vS08/30/321300054. Inspector, Ooishi-san.@k@vS08/30/321300055. ...Chief Inspector Minai's case is not a mere robbery murder.@k@vS08/30/321300056. I refrained from saying anything in front of Madoka-kun, but... that's what I believe."
What did that mean...?
No way... Was this a premeditated crime? Was she targeted...?
"Right. ...She was probably killed...@k@vS08/30/321300058. because of her involvement in this case."
There were no flowers decorating the empty desk. The detectives of the First Investigation Division reached a consensus and forbade anyone from placing a vase on her desk...
"...Listen up, everyone!@k@vS08/30/321300060. Everyone is forbidden from attending Assistant Director Minai's funeral!@k@vS08/30/321300061. ...And please refrain from visiting the grave site!"
"I don't think any of you are entitled to mourn the Assistant Director's death until we've caught the criminal who stabbed her and brought them forth to face judgment!!"
""Uuoooooohh!!""
All the men responded to Chief Yamaoki's declaration by sobbing, closing their eyes, clenching their teeth, and turning red in the face.
The two detectives she trusted most among them, Fujita and Hanada, seemed to be particularly grief-stricken.
"Damn it... Damn iiit!!"
"I'll kill you!@k@vS08/31/320900071. ...I'll show everyone involved in the Chief Inspector's murder that they picked a fight with the wrong people!!"
"Fucking bastards...!!@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1042. Don't expect to live long enough to stand in court!!!"

The two detectives, who seemed mild mannered when I first met them, had a fire in their eyes. They let out a forceful shout as they reached for the handguns on their holsters and jumped up without looking back.
And not just them, detectives of all ages charged together with their weapons to hunt down the Chief Inspector's murderer.
(She... meant a lot to them.)
The true value of a person can only be understood after their death. In that sense, it became abundantly clear that Chief Inspector Minai was an exceptional police officer...
"Inspector Akasaka, Ooishi-san..."
After seeing off the detectives of the First Investigation Division, Chief Yamaoki invited us into another room. In there, he told us the details of the incident.

"The incident occurred around 5 PM.@k@vS08/30/321300065. A truck driver overheard something resembling a fight and went to investigate, then found two men in work clothes struggling with a woman beside the car."
"The truck driver rushed to a nearby phone booth to call the police, then returned to the scene and found her bleeding in the driver's seat.@k@vS08/30/321300067. ...However."
"However...?"
"According to the forensics report, the pattern of blood on the ground indicated that the Chief Inspector was first stabbed in the parking space on the right side of the car."
"...Their car was probably parked there.@k@vS08/30/321300070. And traces of the bloodstains would have been left on their tires..."
...This was Chief Yamaoki's opinion.
The perpetrator probably asked Chief Inspector Minai for help... She probably parked next to the car that was having trouble and offered to give it a look.
And right then, when she was completely unprotected, she was stabbed from behind with a sharp knife, then collapsed to the ground.
...Then she used her remaining strength to get up and fight with the criminals who ransacked her car.
...Which irritated one of the culprits, so they stabbed her again...
I imagined the sight.
Even after the assailant stabbed Chief Inspector Minai... she probably realized that the criminal might try to take Madoka-san's bankbook and resisted as much as she could to protect it.
It may not have had a lot of money, but even so, it was important to her and carried a lot of sentimental value.
So she took it back from them, with no regard for the danger... and in doing so, ended up losing her life...
"But... why do you think it wasn't just theft?"

"...They rifled through her handbag and the glove compartment, and took her wallet and small change.@k@vS08/30/321300072. So far, that's fine.@k@vS08/30/321300073. The problem is, why did they target her car specifically?"
Unable to understand the meaning behind Chief Yamaoki's question, Ooishi-san and I turned to face each other.
A lone woman seemed like an easy target for a thief. Plus, Chief Inspector Minai was driving an unmarked police car, so there was no way for a stranger to know that she was a police officer.
Wait a minute.
That's what a stranger would think, so then...?!
"In that case..."
"Yes.@k@vS08/30/321300075. The criminals must have noticed the police radio when they vandalized the car."
"And of course, they would have noticed the handgun in the glove compartment.@k@vS08/30/321300077. They rifled through everything when they opened it."
"...Yet, they didn't steal the handgun and they didn't search Chief Inspector Minai's clothes to take her police badge.@k@vS08/30/321300079. ...Doesn't that sound unusual?"
"...Indeed, that's strange."
Even if the thief suddenly panicked when they realized their victim was a detective... taking the badge and handgun would be some consolation for the trouble.
The police would furiously investigate the perpetrator in a cop killing incident, so it would be natural to take anything they could use for protection.
But they didn't do that. Were so desperate they could only take the money...?
"Moreover, they took some of the files and documents she had."

"She was the sort of person who wouldn't carry around materials unrelated to the case at hand, so it had to be someone who would benefit from those files.@k@vS08/30/321300082. ...That seems to be the case."
"...Then these documents are the most likely cause?@k@vS08/13/321000745. So from the beginning... the culprit was planning to kill Chief Inspector Minai?!"
Chief Yamaoki's eyebrows wrinkled as he quietly nodded.
...The logic behind it was sound.

"...But how were they able to find out what Tomoe-chan was doing?@k@vS08/11/321100634. It would be meaningless to set up an ambush if they didn't know that Tomoe would stop at the Kakiuchi service area, right?"
"They must have set something up.@k@vS08/30/321300084. ...Something to make certain she'd stop at the service area."
Rather than spend the time explaining it to us, Chief Yamaoki pointed to the whiteboard above the desk.
It was the First Investigation Division's scheduling chart.

"You can identify Chief Inspector Minai's destination by looking at this division's schedule board.@k@vS08/30/321300086. She wrote here that she was heading to the Gifu prefectural police forensics department."
"I noticed that the fuel gauge in her car was completely topped off.@k@vS08/30/321300088. In other words, that proves Chief Inspector Minai stopped at the gas station in the service area to refuel."
"...In fact, according to a report from Hayami-kun, a clerk at the shop saw her there pumping gas shortly before the incident occurred."
"...Why did she refuel there?"
"Maybe it was the Gifu prefectural police, or maybe it was someone else... but I believe someone siphoned the gas out of her car while she was there."
"In fact, if she was low on fuel when she left the police station, she would've had to refuel somewhere on the way."
Chief Minai was a thrifty person. If she needed to refuel, she would've opted to use the cheapest gas station around.
"Besides, there are regional differences in the gas tax, so it's cheaper here than it is in the neighboring prefecture."
"It's a long distance south of here before the next opportunity to refuel, so it's quite crowded with travelers heading that direction.@k@vS08/28/320500975. I stop here too, from time to time."

"And... I checked the recordings at the information center earlier, but there were no reports from Chief Inspector Minai.@k@vS08/30/321300093. That didn't sit well with me since she usually reports in frequently, so I did some digging...@k@vS08/30/321300094. and there were apparently signs the tape was overwritten."
Chief Yamaoki set down a tape recorder and hit play.
Silence continued for three to four minutes, as if nothing was recorded... but just as I thought that,
"...Heading that way. Over."
"...Roger, over and out."
That was unmistakably the voice of Chief Inspector Minai.
"...That means someone erased the content of the tape?!"
"Right. I'm going to turn this over to the forensic investigation team at prefectural police headquarters and see if it's possible to recover the missing audio."
"H-hold on...!@k@vS08/11/321100637. Then, the person who killed her...?!"

"There must be a traitor here in this station.@k@vS08/30/321300097. ...Simple logic points to that.@k@vS08/30/321300098. And there must have been an accomplice at her destination.@k@vS08/30/321300099. ...Essentially, that's what I've come to believe."
"...Heheh, heheheheh...!"
A light chuckle echoed through the room. ...It was Ooishi-san's voice.
"...Akasaka-san.@k@vS08/11/321100640. With this... I have one more reason to kill the people behind this curse...!"
"Ooishi-san..."
"I'll never forgive them for killing her in such an terrible way...!!@k@vS08/11/321100642. Right! I'll do it! ...I'll hunt them to the ends of the earth!"

"Until I can't move any longer, until all life has been exhausted from my body!@k@vS08/11/321100644. I'll follow these jackasses and find them!@k@vS08/11/321100645. Then I'll beat them until they're bloody and on death's door, and then I'll put them in prison!!"
Ooishi-san lit up like a raging inferno, repeatedly smashing his fist into the wall.
Even though I was holding back my emotions... I felt the same way.
"...Also, one of the emergency responders heard her final words.@k@vS08/30/321300101. ...She said, "Shiro, cabinet office."@k@vS08/30/321300102. She repeated it three times."
"Shiro... cabinet office?"
What did that mean?
The words "shiro" and "cabinet office" had no special meaning, to my knowledge...
"The word "shiro" means white, and may refer to the color.@k@vS08/30/321300104. But this "cabinet office"...@k@vS08/30/321300105. I'll let you know as soon as I learn anything more."
"...Thank you."
July 8th, Showa 58 (Friday).
changeReason: Date format changed from Western calendar year to Japanese Showa era dating, which alters how the reader contextualizes the time period.

TextENGNew: "...Haah."
Heading out of the break room around 9 o'clock, I stepped into the First Investigation Division's main office.
I looked around the interior.
There were no signs of anyone from the division at work. ...Maybe they worked all night and couldn't get home until very late.
Was this room always so large?
...I examined the room as I thought that, then my gaze stopped at a certain point... and a sense of loneliness welled up inside me.
"...Ooishi-san's... still asleep, huh?"
We were here late last night... organizing investigation related information until dawn.
About the relationship between the Great Hinamizawa Disaster and the Gifu prefectural police headquarters that Chief Inspector Minai visited yesterday...

However, there was nothing relating it to the "fifth year curse"... other than the nurse burning incident. I also contacted the Gifu prefectural police forensics team, but their only response was "she didn't come here."
And her trustworthy contact there, Matsuoka, was out on a sudden business trip and wouldn't return until next week. ...So, we decided to take a short rest in the break room to cool our heads.
Ooishi-san was a little late, probably because he had trouble falling asleep...
Just then, I heard a knock from behind the door.
"...Come in?"
I replied even though I wasn't sure if it was okay for me to do so. When the door opened, a middle-aged female police officer stood before me.
"Umm... excuse me.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_PoliFa1002. Is there someone named Ooishi-san here?"
"Huh...? Ah, yes.@k@vS08/13/321000758. Did you need something?"
"Oh, yes.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_PoliFa1004. This package is for Ooishi-san... It's a special delivery from Chief Inspector Minai."
I was so surprised, I kicked the trash can at my feet as I stood up.
Then I rushed over to the female officer and snatched the large envelope from her hands to look at the sender and recipient.
"Ah, hold on...?"
Indeed, it was addressed to Kakiuchi Station and the name "Kuraudo Ooishi-sama" was written in clear, feminine handwriting.
There were only a handful of people in the office who knew that he was here. Yet...
"...I'm sorry for putting you on the spot.@k@vS08/13/321000763. Is there anyone here who would know if this looks like Chief Inspector Minai's handwriting?"
"Huh?@k@vS08/00/VT2B_PoliFa1007. Oh, yes, I would, if you don't mind.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_PoliFa1008. ...This certainly does look like her handwriting."
Even though the female officer was confused at first, she quickly regained her composure like a true veteran and answered my question.
...She told me that she worked as a postal clerk for over three years, so she had ample experience analyzing handwriting to deal with forgeries and practical jokes.
And since she, herself, knew about Chief Inspector Minai's murder, she inspected it carefully to confirm for herself.
...Still thinking it's strange, I decided to show it to the either the section chief of the First Investigation Division or his representative.
"Excuse me.@k@vS08/13/321000765. Please take a look at this.@k@vS08/13/321000766. And, could you tell me the extension number of the receptionist by the break room?"
I held up my police badge and stated my identity, then picked up the phone and dialed the number I heard from the female officer.
...After it rang several times, I neglected to greet the person on the other end and immediately started talking.
"Please tell a man named Ooishi-san to hurry over to the First Investigation Division."
"...Okay, I'm opening it."
I got in touch with Chief Yamaoki and Madoka-san, who watched along with Detective Fujita and the others as Ooishi-san opened the large envelope and removed its contents.
...Inside, we found a complete set of documents paperclipped together, a pamphlet, an insurance card... and a letter.
"...Umm, Ooishi-san.@k@vS08/13/321000769. What is all this...?"
Ooishi-san silently handed it over to me. ...Looking it over, it was only a few sentences long.
"I had a bad feeling while refueling my car, so this is just a precaution.@k@vS08/28/320500979. If it turns out to be nothing, please don't laugh too much, ok?"
"No way... Chief Inspector Minai?!"

"...Shocking, eh?@k@vS08/11/321100649. I thought she had a bad habit of being too cautious and paranoid,@k@vS08/11/321100650. ...but this is proof that she was right after all.@k@vS08/11/321100651. She really deserved better...!"
...Chief Inspector Minai probably felt something was odd about how the fuel level in her car suddenly dropped as she waited to finish refueling.
(Maybe... someone did this on purpose?)
Sensing the impending danger... she must have been preparing for a worst-case scenario. She must've smiled bitterly to herself as she wrote the letter, wondering if she was overthinking it.
Indeed, if nothing had happened, it would've been a funny story worthy of a little teasing.
Even so, she acted faithfully and carried out her responsibility as a detective...
"Honestly... that foolish commitment to her duty as an officer...!@k@vS08/29/321200298. Just like Dad...!"
"...Madoka-san."
Then, we looked through the papers she sent.
These were probably copies made with a cheap office copy machine, so they were a little hard to read due to the bleeding of the ink. But they were still legible enough to grasp the contents.
One document was a drug analysis report from the Gifu prefectural police forensics division. ...It bore the seal of the person in charge, "Matsuoka."

"This... is the medical certificate for that medicine capsule.@k@vS08/31/320900073. The one we found at the Hatakeyama house.@k@vS08/31/320900074. ...!@k@vS08/31/320900075. And this one's the analysis results for the pills that belong to Natsumi Kimiyoshi!"

Then Detective Fujita told us what he discussed with Chief Inspector Minai the other day... her reasoning to suspect that the killer in the "Hatakeyama family murder case" was actually their daughter, Aoi Hatakeyama.

Everyone was completely surprised... and Detective Hanada grabbed him in anger, asking, "Why didn't you tell us sooner?"... but he withdrew his hand when he learned she kept it from us because he and I had daughters of our own.
But... these new facts had a huge impact.
"...No way. ...Is this all true?!"
"Yes, there is no mistake.@k@vS08/31/320900077. ...The two drugs are completely identical."
"And these pills contain large quantities of anti-stimulants and mental transmission inhibitant agents.@k@vS08/31/320900079. It says here... they're mainly used in drugs that treat mental illness."
"Mental illness?@k@vS08/13/321000774. ...Both Natsumi Kimiyoshi and Aoi Hatakeyama?!"
"Then... that means the patients taking these have the same mental illness?"
"A specific disease name couldn't be identified. We'd need to check with their doctor."
"...However, the one who wrote this said that due to the potency of this medicine, it's certainly not approved for use in Japan.@k@vS08/31/320900082. ...And it's also banned in Europe and America!"
So... in summary.
Normally, negative emotions like anger, hate, and resentment can be controlled by "reasoning."
However, there are also people who can't control those feelings naturally.
It can't simply be brushed aside as a personality problem. In that sense... both Natsumi Kimiyoshi and Aoi Hatakeyama had some manner of mental disorder.
And based on the components in the medicine, it can be inferred that they lacked the ability to suppress explosions of "anger" and other emotional outburts.

Normally, education and discipline would correct the issue at times like those... but when their symptoms manifested, the two of them probably took this fast-acting medicine to mentally stabilize themselves.
However, the stronger the medicine, the more they grow dependent on it. And the side effects of withdrawal become even more apparent.
For as much as it rapidly suppresses the surge of emotion, the user will instantly suffer a stronger rebound the moment the medicine wears off.
And that can result in heightened destructive urges, development of a persecution complex, or other extreme thoughts and actions...
In other words, these emotional explosions can temporarily drive them into a violent mindset and massively elevate the risk of outlandish behavior...!

"...For the time being, we need to look into Natsumi Kimiyoshi's past.@k@vS08/13/321000777. Her treatment history, her hospitalization history... and her reputation at school would all be valuable clues in assessing her current situation.@k@vS08/13/321000778. Ooishi-san, let's head out right away!"
"Right! Then I'll go check with her old middle school in Okinomiya and..."
Just then.
"Yes, this is conference room 3.@k@vS08/29/321200300. ...Huh? Oh, yes.@k@vS08/29/321200301. ...Akasaka-san, you have a call from Tokyo.@k@vS08/29/321200302. From someone named Kanou-san."
Hearing that name left me confused. ...After all, Kanou-san told me that he wouldn't contact me during the investigation.
Then, why...?
"...Would you like to take the call in another room, Inspector Akasaka?@k@vS08/30/321300107. You can use my office."
"Thanks a lot.@k@vS08/13/321000781. Alright, I'll head there now."
"But, why did Sis have a pamphlet for a dental clinic...?"
"Hello, this is Akasaka."
The message from Kanou-san was even more shocking than I could've predicted.
"...Wha?!@k@vS08/13/321000784. H-hold on a minute, Kanou-san!!@k@vS08/13/321000785. What does that even mean?!"
"...I meant exactly what I said.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_kanou1086. We received an order to end your investigation activities this week.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_kanou1087. I'm sorry, but I need you to come back."
"...What is it, Chisato-chan?@k@vS08/36/320100274. ...Do you need something?"
I let out an awkward laugh... and glanced at Natsumi to assess her reaction.
Natsumi quizzically tilted her head... It looked like she had a slightly... no, a very unpleasant look on her face.
(Just as I thought... This isn't normal.)
Her face never looked like that when I called out to her at school.
She'd act a little surprised and restless, yet would flash me a friendly smile.
She would display childish innocence, or joy that someone was talking to her... She's as cute as a baby animal.
I really like that part of Natsumi, so I would tease her and stick to her like glue even though I felt bad about it and knew the others were probably sick of it.
But... she's clearly been acting unusual lately.
She doesn't play along with my teasing and jokes, and is slow to react. And whenever I try to get up in her personal space, she looks clearly displeased.
...At first, I thought she was growing weary of our daily routine... but that's not it.
When she talks to us... she sounds more and more apathetic.
In fact, Tamako has asked her several times, "Are you ok?" and, "Are you feeling sick?" but... she hardly has anything to say these days.
When we asked her why, she just said "...I'm a little irritated."
...It seems like Tamako has had bad feelings toward Natsumi since the incident the other day. ...Apparently, she still hasn't heard the full story from Akira.
Regardless, there are times when everyone is in a bad mood and would rather not talk about it, so it's better to tread lightly...
However...
(Irritated... huh?)
Thinking about it, Natsumi sometimes felt down when bad things happened, but she never took it out on anyone else.
In fact, she was more likely to apologize for her depression bringing down the people around her...
Things had a tendency to accumulate inside her, so it was always a lot of work to find out what was on her mind and cheer her up.
But, now...
"Do you want anything, Chisato-chan?"
"No... it's nothing. I just called out because I saw you..."
"...I see."
After saying that.
Natsumi seemed annoyed as she let out a sigh, then continued heading down the school walkway.
I was discouraged for a moment as I stared at the coldness in her back, but quickly resolved myself. And then,
"Na... Natsumi!"
I ran over to her as fast I could and grabbed her hand to stop her.
Seeing nothing but darkness in the back of her eyes, I suddenly caught my breath.
Is this... really the face of Natsumi?
No.
We're the ones who drove Natsumi to this point.
No doubt, this is our doing...
...I noticed something was off starting with that incident the other day.
That time when she spoke to Akira in front of the vending machines... and she suddenly got angry and shouted. Afterwards, I asked Akira about what happened.
"In the classroom... we were discussing a special feature article from a magazine.@k@vS08/39/320200031. You know, the one about the incidents caused by former residents of Hinamizawa village..."
"Aah... that."
My father showed me that article when I was eating breakfast at home.

From what I remember, the culprits behind those bizarre incidents believed in their local god, "Oyashiro-sama," and started decorating their houses with weird charms and magic spells... and things like that.
Maybe Dad let me read it because he thought it was a tasteless little joke...
But since I knew that one of my friends was potentially from Hinamizawa, I found myself unable to laugh about it. Honestly, it terrified me...

"Rumor has it, the recent incident at West School was caused by a student originally from Hinamizawa...@k@vS08/39/320200033. As they were talking about it, someone in class said they saw something in our high school area.@k@vS08/39/320200034. And then..."
"Saw what?"
"The house in one of the magazine photos, decorated with charms.@k@vS08/39/320200036. There were rumors, but nothing concrete, so one of the girls asked where it was..."
"...Oyashiro-sama believers?"
"Yeah, it's just a rumor... but I saw it in a magazine.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_StuFb1002. It said... the area where the believers of Oyashiro-sama live is..."
"Really? That's in our school district.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_StuFa1003. Seriously?!"
"Supposedly, there are people who actually saw it.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_StuFb1004. A house decorated with talismans like that..."
"Oh, I heard that too!@k@vS08/00/VT2B_StuMa1002. ...And did you hear the person behind the West School incident was from Hinamizawa?"

"Huh? The one where a student was waving a knife in the classroom?@k@vS08/00/VT2B_StuFb1006. That was someone from Hinamizawa...?@k@vS08/00/VT2B_StuFb1007. No way... Are there any students like that here too?"
"Hey, wait a minute..."
"...Hey, Kimiyoshi-san, Toudou-kun.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_StuFa1005. Have either of you seen a house like this nearby?"

"There are rumors that it's in this school district, but I don't know where.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_StuFa1007. It looks so sketchy, so if you've seen it, you'll know immediately...@k@vS08/00/VT2B_StuFa1008. So what about you, Toudou-kun?"
"...How did you answer that?"
"How did you answer?!@k@vS08/37/320300200. It's really important. Please answer properly!"
""I-I don't know anything.@k@vS08/39/320200039. It's a trashy story. I'm not interested."..."
Right then... I rushed forward and slapped Akira's cheek with the back of my hand.
"Ow?@k@vS08/39/320200041. Wh-what was that for, Chisa...?"
Naturally, he angrily glared back at me after receiving a slapping. ...But, his face quickly turned to bewilderment, and the anger in his voice washed away.
Because...
For the first time in a long time... I began shedding tears.
"Chisa...?"
"You... you said that?@k@vS08/37/320300203. Akira, you said that right in front of Natsumi?!"
I grabbed Akira by the collar and barked at him as I moved in closer as though I were about to bite him.
...It's fine for classmates to say irresponsible things about this. They don't matter!
But... but!!
But for the man she loved, the person she so innocently adored... to say something so awful to her...!!
So frustrating.
It's so frustrating that I can't tell him the truth!
But if he knew why I was upset... what would happen with him?
And what would happen with Natsumi?!
So I can't say anything... I can't say any more than that!
But... but...!!
"Chisato...@k@vS08/39/320200045. No way, you too?"
But, after those few words.
I reacted by lifting my head, my tears scattering.
Just now... Akira said "you too" right...?
No way, could he...?
Should I say something?
Should I stay silent like this...? My mind ran cold as I considered it, and hesitation clouded my thoughts.
If I were to rashly confess the truth to Akira when I know nothing about the situation... what would he think of Natsumi?
Would he be afraid?
Would he find it creepy? ...Would it wipe away his previous feelings?
If I tell Akira the whole story, it could tear their relationship apart.
I'd be betraying Natsumi...
"Chisato... what do you know?@k@vS08/39/320200047. Tell me!"
"Hey, Chisa!!"
I've decided.
I... have to protect her.
No matter how much I'm hated... if it's for that, I can accept it.
Even if it kills me...
"Hey, come on!"
"...Promise me."

"Promise me.@k@vS08/37/320300210. ...Even after you hear this, promise you won't hate Natsumi.@k@vS08/37/320300211. That girl... Natsumi really is a nice, kind-hearted girl.@k@vS08/37/320300212. ...Believe that, until the end!"
"...Chisato."
For a split second.
Akira gasped and tensed up as if he couldn't understand what I was saying.
But... really, it was just for a moment.
He immediately looked back at me and nodded, saying "Yeah" with a look of determination.
All right... nicely done.
With that... I can give in to you.
As I muttered that to myself, I released my grip on his collar... and took a step back to put some distance between us.
"...The town where Natsumi lived before she moved here is called Okinomiya. It's right next to Hinamizawa.@k@vS08/37/320300214. And I think her family is... somehow related to Hinamizawa."
Akira seemed a little surprised. His eyes opened wide and he bit his lip.
Yet, his reaction was moderate enough that I was convinced.
"Akira... you know something too, don't you?"
"...When I was escorting Kimiyoshi home earlier... I saw it.@k@vS08/39/320200054. It was decorated with those weird dolls, the creepy straw festoons, and those prayers written across the wall..."
"...I see."
So I guess Akira already realized that Natsumi was from Hinamizawa?
No... hold on.
Then why would he call it "trashy" and say he's "not interested"...?
"...I didn't want anyone asking Kimiyoshi any more about Hinamizawa.@k@vS08/39/320200056. Plus, I feared if they kept talking about it, someone might start to suspect it was her house....."
"...Akira, you may be good at painting, but your social skills leave something to be desired..."
I felt a little relieved to learn that he didn't do it out of malice or ignorance, but out of consideration for Natsumi... As I began to grasp the flow of the story, I let out a sigh.
In all likelihood... Natsumi probably misunderstood the meaning behind Akira's words when he described her troubles as "trashy" and "uninteresting," so she must've been terribly shocked.
So, she began feeling more and more alienated, and she shut herself in...
Learning that Akira felt that way made her feel hopelessly alone.
"Did I... say something terrible to Kimiyoshi...?"
"...Normally, that wouldn't be the case.@k@vS08/37/320300219. But she's been so nervous lately, so she might've taken it especially hard..."
Akira looked deeply troubled as he scratched his head.
...However, he was such a poor communicator, so it would be too much to expect him to cheer Natsumi up, no matter how hard he tried.
In that case, I'll have to...
"...I'll try talking to Natsumi.@k@vS08/37/320300221. I'll deal with all this somehow."
"Chisato..."
"I don't know if it'll go well, though.@k@vS08/37/320300223. ...No, I'll make it go well.@k@vS08/37/320300224. And when she's back to normal, I'll leave the rest to you, Akira-kun."
"Don't give me that!@k@vS08/37/320300226. I'm asking you to become a proper boyfriend and support her!@k@vS08/37/320300227. You got it?!"
"Y-yeah. Got it...@k@vS08/39/320200062. I won't make the same mistake again.@k@vS08/39/320200063. ...I promise!"
Akira seemed confused and overwhelmed at first, but my conviction must have gotten through to him. He gave me a powerful nod of determination.
"And... tell Tamako everything you just told me, too.@k@vS08/37/320300229. Ever since Natsumi started acting strange, she's been seriously worried about her...@k@vS08/37/320300230. That's all I ask."
"...Understood."
Alright. ...From here on, everything depends on my hard work.
That's how I felt... all the way until now.
I managed to secure a location for Natsumi and I to talk alone, but what on earth should I say to her...?
"...Chisato-chan?"
"Huh?"
"You're hurting me.@k@vS08/36/320100280. Would you let go?"
In response to Natsumi's curt accusation, I slowly pulled my hand away.
I was worried she might leave the moment I let go... but Natsumi turned to me and sighed as though I was being a nuisance.
"...So, did you need something?"

"Oh... yeah.@k@vS08/37/320300234. N-Natsumi, it seems like you haven't been feeling well lately.@k@vS08/37/320300235. So, I invited Tamako today, and I was thinking the three of us would hit the town together again.@k@vS08/37/320300236. Ah, ahahaha..."
"...That's nice, but I can't.@k@vS08/36/320100283. I have something to do today."
She answered immediately.
And even though she said she had "something to do," she wouldn't say what it was. ...That wasn't the sort of thing Natsumi would do before now.
"Well then.@k@vS08/37/320300238. Ah... how about tomorrow?@k@vS08/37/320300239. Tomorrow is Saturday, so you'll have plenty of time, right?"

"Oh right, you have your part-time job at the care center tomorrow.@k@vS08/37/320300241. I can call the car for you. Would you like a ride?@k@vS08/37/320300242. It's faster than taking the train, plus..."
"...You're being annoying."
That moment.
I couldn't believe what she just said.
Annoying...?
Natsumi said that about me...?
"Didn't you hear me?@k@vS08/36/320100287. I said you're being annoying."
"Na-Natsumi..."
"To be blunt, you're irritating me.@k@vS08/36/320100289. Every time I see you, you try clinging to me or telling stupid jokes all for your own amusement."
"Could you stop getting all buddy-buddy with me and acting like you're my friend?@k@vS08/36/320100291. I'm not your toy, you know..."
"Wh... what are you saying...?"
I couldn't believe it.
Was this really Natsumi?
Could the person I'm talking to now really be the Natsumi Kimiyoshi I loved so much?!
I was so shocked, it felt like my head got smacked with a huge hammer. It was so unsettling that all the blood in my body froze over.
This... couldn't simply be handwaved as her being in a bad mood.
The girl with those terrifyingly cold eyes standing before me was completely different from the Natsumi I knew.
"Na-Natsumi, do you understand what you're saying?"

"We're friends, aren't we?@k@vS08/37/320300248. Is it really so strange for friends to act so close to one another?@k@vS08/37/320300249. Well, I may have gone overboard a couple times and done some strange things, but even so..."
"Even so... we're friends?@k@vS08/36/320100293. Who?@k@vS08/36/320100294. Why?@k@vS08/36/320100295. Ahahahahahahaha."
"Natsumi...!"

"Well then.@k@vS08/36/320100297. If you're a friend, you can say anything?@k@vS08/36/320100298. If you're a friend, you're allowed to do anything?@k@vS08/36/320100299. ...Being a "friend" sure is convenient, isn't it?"

"Friend, friend, f r i e n d, f r i e n d...!@k@vS08/36/320100301. Disgusting, nauseating!!@k@vS08/36/320100302. How can you call a person who uses someone as a stepping stone for their own amusement a friend?!@k@vS08/36/320100303. Don't make me laugh!!!"
"Kn... knock it off, Natsumi!!@k@vS08/37/320300252. When have I ever used you as a stepping stone?!"
Suddenly.
Natsumi's eyes quietly narrowed.
Even as her sharp eyes bore into me, I pushed myself hard to keep going.

"I, well, I've liked you since I first met you!@k@vS08/37/320300254. You looked like you were in a tight spot, so I wanted to help! ...Seriously!@k@vS08/37/320300255. I'm pleased to hear you say "thanks," and even when you say "sorry" I don't mind it at all!!"
"I was delighted when you relied on me, and that made me like you more and more!!@k@vS08/37/320300257. Even now!!!"

"Even recently, when you were suffering through exam prep, I dropped all my plans to help you out, didn't I?@k@vS08/37/320300259. And when you were thrilled when we found out you did well, it made me extremely happy!!"

"And when Akira confessed to you, the three of us celebrated together, didn't we?@k@vS08/37/320300261. Because I knew that Akira was a nice guy!@k@vS08/37/320300262. Because he's my childhood friend!"
"And when I found out that you fell in love with him, I was as glad as if it'd happened to me, really...!!"
Even so... Natsumi continued staring at me without breaking away from her cold expression. And then,
"...Hmm.@k@vS08/36/320100308. Then, was choosing that college also for my sake...?"

"Chisato-chan... you're going to Rikuo University, right?@k@vS08/36/320100310. Together with Tamako-chan.@k@vS08/36/320100311. And you know that Toudou-kun is going too.@k@vS08/36/320100312. ...But in my case, you told me I'd never get accepted and stood in my way...!!"
"No, I never stood in your way..."
"You diiid!!!"
...Just like that.
Natsumi raised her voice... with the force of a beast biting into the throat of its prey.
Upon seeing that brutal display of rage... the passion in my heart that inspired me to keep going immediately collapsed in on itself...
"Na-Natsumi... what are you saying...?!"
"You were planning to separate me from Toudou-kun from the start, weren't you?@k@vS08/36/320100315. I know.@k@vS08/36/320100316. I heard from a classmate."

"...Chisato-chan, you've always liked Toudou-kun, ever since elementary school.@k@vS08/36/320100318. You tried to confess to him several times, but it never got through to him, so you got depressed every time, right?"
"Ahahahahaha, what a failure!"
"Y-you're wrong..."
"...And when I transferred here and he suddenly took an interest in me, you were jealous, weren't you?"

"When you said we should celebrate his confession to me, you were glaring daggers at me. You think I didn't notice?@k@vS08/36/320100322. You were eavesdropping on us the whole time, outside of the classroom, right?@k@vS08/36/320100323. Then you took me around town and treated me like some country bumpkin, like an idiot."
"Well sorry for being a country bumpkin.@k@vS08/36/320100325. Did that make you feel any better?@k@vS08/36/320100326. Ahahahahahahaha."
"Th-that's not true at all..."
"...You did.@k@vS08/36/320100328. You absolutely did.@k@vS08/36/320100329. I know.@k@vS08/36/320100330. You made a fool of me!!"
After hearing that... I couldn't say anything, so I remained silent.
I wanted to say she was wrong.
I wanted to deny ever even considering something like that.
But... I couldn't. Because Tamako repeatedly told me that I was subconsciously doing things that could be interpreted that way.
But... No, because of that...
Seeing the look of condemnation of Natsumi's face gouged at my heart and made my throat feel painfully tight.
"Worried about me?@k@vS08/36/320100332. Thinking about me?@k@vS08/36/320100333. Give me a break. In the end, you just wanted to show me how great you are, right?!"

"...Aah, you said I was cute.@k@vS08/36/320100335. But you meant that I was cute like a pet, right?@k@vS08/36/320100336. So you couldn't forgive that your pet was stealing your favorite person?@k@vS08/36/320100337. Isn't that it?"
"Ahahahahaha.@k@vS08/36/320100339. That's too bad!"
"I'm not a pet, I'm a human being!!@k@vS08/36/320100341. I won't live the way you want me to! Don't think you can trick me forever!!!"
"I-I thought you were suffering because of Hinamizawa!@k@vS08/37/320300272. S-so I..."
"...@k@vS08/36/320100343. Who told you that?"
By the time I realized I'd blurted out something I shouldn't have... it was already too late.
Natsumi opened her eyes wide in disbelief for a moment... but then she looked at me with scornful eyes, with contempt from the bottom of her heart.
Stop it... Stop it, Natsumi...!
That awful expression... I don't want to see it on you... I don't want to see it!
"...I see now.@k@vS08/36/320100345. So you know about me too.@k@vS08/36/320100346. Hmm.@k@vS08/36/320100347. Hehehehe, ahahahahahaha..."
"I-I'm worried about you... really!@k@vS08/37/320300275. I'm scared you might get caught up in one of those strange incidents too... so that's why...!"
"...So, who did you tell about that?@k@vS08/36/320100349. The teacher?@k@vS08/36/320100350. Classmates?@k@vS08/36/320100351. Or maybe... Toudou-kun?"
"Don't... don't get the wrong idea!!@k@vS08/37/320300277. Do I really seem like that kind of person?!"

"Yeah, you do.@k@vS08/36/320100353. I can't believe you.@k@vS08/36/320100354. You betrayed me.@k@vS08/36/320100355. ...Telling the person you betrayed to believe in you. Who would believe that?@k@vS08/36/320100356. Ahahahahahaha."
"Na-Natsumi...!"
"Is that all you wanted to discuss?@k@vS08/36/320100358. How boring.@k@vS08/36/320100359. Anything more would be a waste of time."
Then, Natsumi quickly walked into the school building without looking back.
I didn't have the strength to follow her, so I collapsed to my knees on the spot... I put my hands on the ground and hung my head in shame.
One, two... a trail of droplets fell and left dark stains on the concrete.
"You're wrong... you're wrong...!@k@vS08/37/320300280. I wouldn't... I would never do that... to you...!"
But no matter how many excuses I made... it didn't make a difference.
Was I in the wrong after all?
Because I did something unbecoming of a friend?
But... but in that case, what should I have done?!
Friends... what does that even mean? What is a friend?
What does it mean to be a friend...?!
